title_en: Look Closer: Pieter De Hooch’s Puzzle Box
title_zh: 看得更近：皮特·德霍赫的“拼图箱”
rubric_en: What the painter, a contemporary of Vermeer, revealed and concealed in a beguiling 1663 work
rubric_zh: 这位与维米尔同时代的画家，在一幅迷人的1663年作品中既展示又隐藏了什么

paragraphs.en: Pieter de Hooch was a contemporary of Johannes Vermeer in the Dutch city of Delft for a time; they painted similar subjects, in similar costumes, engaged in similarly quotidian activities. But they were quite different artists. De Hooch’s 1663 painting Interior With Women Beside a Linen Cupboard delivers exactly as little drama and numinous transcendence as its title promises. (It was formerly called The Good Housewife, which is hardly better.) The intrigue lies elsewhere.
paragraphs.zh: 皮特·德霍赫曾在荷兰代尔夫特与约翰内斯·维梅尔同时代活动一段时间；他们描绘了相似的题材、穿着相似的服装，并把人物放在同样寻常的日常情境中。但他们其实是两种完全不同的艺术家。德霍赫的1663年作品《室内的女性与亚麻木柜》几乎没有标题所说的戏剧性或神圣超脱感。（它过去曾被称为《好主妇》，这名字也毫不见得更好。）真正值得玩味的地方在别处。

paragraphs.en: De Hooch’s picture is a puzzle box—an ingenious construction of openings and closings, insides and outsides, revelation and concealment. The sturdy wall behind the standing women with their crisply folded stack of linen is breached in three different places, extending our vista with sudden depth. On the right is a stairway twisting up and out of sight, on the left a window, and in the center a door.
paragraphs.zh: 德霍赫的这幅画是一座“谜题盒”——一个精妙构成的结构，充满了开与合、内与外、显露与隐藏。站立着的女性身后、她们那摞整齐折叠的亚麻布前面的厚实墙壁，在三个不同位置被突破，从而让我们的视野骤然向更深处延展。右侧是一段台阶，盘旋向上并消失在视线之外；左侧是一扇窗；中间则是一扇门。

paragraphs.en: From the May 2023 issue: How to look at a Vermeer
paragraphs.zh: 选自2023年5月号：《如何观赏维梅尔》

paragraphs.en: These last two open onto the voorhuis, a foyer punctuated with a second, taller window and another doorway, beyond which we can see a sunlit snippet of the outside world—a bit of tree, the suggestion of a canal, and a building on the opposite side, with its own syncopated grid of windows, doors, and brickwork. (Look again at the spot of sky, diced by overlapping panes of glass, and you might catch a glimpse of the light and structure, the clarity and enigma, of Piet Mondrian.)
paragraphs.zh: 这两处通道通向前厅（voorhuis），这是一个带有第二扇更高窗户和另一扇门廊的前室，在那扇门之后，我们可以看见一段被阳光照亮的室外景象——一点树影、运河的轮廓，以及对岸的一栋建筑，带有它自己断续排列的窗户、门和砖墙纹理。（再看一次那片天空吧，被重叠的玻璃片切割成小块；你可能会捕捉到某种光与结构、清晰与谜团交织的感觉——像皮特·蒙德里安一样。）

paragraphs.en: Our attention is being endlessly redirected. The brightest things in the picture—that bit of blue heaven and the red-and-white house across the canal—are also the most distant. Meanwhile, the one piece of incipient action is hidden in shadow: a child of 5 or 6, standing on the threshold between what we can see clearly and what we can’t, with a kolf stick cocked to send a small ball straight out of the picture and into our world.
paragraphs.zh: 我们的注意力总是被不断地引导着转移。画面中最醒目的地方——那一抹蓝色天空和运河对岸红白相间的房屋——却也是最遥远的。与此同时，唯一一处刚刚开始的动作却隐藏在阴影里：一个5到6岁的孩子站在我们看得清楚与看不清楚之间的门槛上，端着一支高尔夫球杆，似乎正要把一颗小球直接击打出画面，进入我们的世界。

paragraphs.en: From the December 1972 issue: The inside story of the Mellon art collection
paragraphs.zh: 发表于1972年12月刊：梅隆艺术收藏的内情

paragraphs.en: Looked at one way, De Hooch’s scene is assertively ordinary. Looked at another way, it’s a lesson in the limits of visibility and knowledge. There’s the rectilinear orderliness of floor tiles and bricks, limpid windowpanes, and perfectly folded fabric. And there’s mayhem, writ small, in the unpredictable trajectories of a child and a ball.
paragraphs.zh: 从一个角度看，德·霍赫的这个场景显得十分果断地普通。换个角度看，它又像是一堂关于可见性和认知边界的课程。地砖和砖墙的几何秩序、清澈透明的窗玻璃、以及褶皱平整的织物，构成了秩序的表面；而一个孩子和一颗球不可预测的轨迹，则在其中掀起了缩小版的混乱。

paragraphs.en: This article appears in the August 2026 print edition with the headline “Interior With Women Beside a Linen Cupboard.”
paragraphs.zh: 本文刊登于2026年8月印刷版，标题为“室内：女性与亚麻壁柜相伴”。
